{"step_by_step_thinking": "Brain death is defined as the irreversible cessation of all functions of the entire brain, including the brainstem. Signs of brain death include the absence of brainstem reflexes and the inability to maintain vital functions like breathing. Positive spinal reflexes on stimulation, sweating, and tachycardia can still be present in brain death due to spinal cord function. Decoicate and decerebrate posturing are abnormal posturing movements that can occur in brain death due to brainstem dysfunction. Normal blood pressure without pharmacological support is not a sign of brain death, as the brainstem controls autonomic functions including blood pressure regulation.", "answer_choice": "A"}
